nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A mutual exclusion algorithm for N processes where at most one process is in the critical section at a time.

Hints: Should define processes as 1..N, track state as {idle, trying, critical}.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE MutualExclusion ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N   (* number of processes *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Variables -----------------------------------------------------------

VARIABLES procState    (* mapping from each process id to its current state *), procState

vars == <<procState>>

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* State definitions -----------------------------------------------

Idle      == "idle"
Trying    == "trying"
Critical  == "critical"

StatesSet == { Idle , Trying , Critical }

ProcessIds == DOMAIN [i |-> i IN 1 .. N]

TypeOk ==
     /\ Domain(procState) = ProcessIds
     /\  \A  p : p \in ProcessIds => 
          procState[p] \in StatesSet

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Initial condition ----------------------------------------------

Init ==
     TypeOk /\
     (\E c \in ProcessIds :
        (/\ procState[c] #= Critical
         /\ UNCHANGED <<(UNUSED)>>))

(* The initial assignment ensures that no process starts already in the critical section.  
   All other states are unconstrained but must be one of the allowed values.
*)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Next-state relation ---------------------------------------------

Next ==
     \/ TRYING_TO_CRITICAL
     \/ CRIT_EXITED
     \/ Terminating

TRYING_TO_CRITICAL ==
       LET P == CHOOSE p WHERE procState[p] = Idle
           Qs == {q: q \in ProcessIds & q /= P}
       IN
            /\ procState'[P] = Trying
            /\ UnchangedExcept(P)


                            ((o <> P) /\ (procState[o] = Critical))
       IN
            /\ !OthersInCrit  (* ensure at most one can enter *)
            /\ procState'[P] = Critical
            /\ UnchangedExcept(P)

CRIT_EXITED ==
      LET C == CHOICE p : procState[p] = Critical 
          InTryOther != FALSE
              ==  \E  t \in ProcessIds :
                    ((t #= C)
                     /\\ procState[t] = Trying )
      IN
        /\ UNCHANGED <<(UNUSED)>>
        /\ IF InTryOther THEN
             /* If another process is trying, it may proceed to critical */
             TRUE   (* placeholder for potential transition actual logic omitted *)

             /* No other processes are in the "trying" state – release CS */
             procState'[C] = Idle

Terminating ==
     /\ UNCHANGED vars

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

====

repaired_spec:
---- MODULE MutualExclusion ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets
CONSTANTS N

VARIABLES state, flag, turn

(* State of each process: idle, trying, critical *)
State == {"idle", "trying", "critical"}

(* Initial state: all processes idle, no flags set, turn arbitrary *)
Init == /\ state \in [1..N -> State]
        /\ flag \in [1..N -> BOOLEAN]
        /\ turn \in 1..N
        /\ \A i \in 1..N: state[i] = "idle" /\ flag[i] = FALSE

(* Next-state relation: process i can move from idle to trying, from trying to critical if turn = i, from critical to idle *)
Next == \E i \in 1..N:
          \/ /\ state[i] = "idle"
             /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "trying"]
             /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![i] = TRUE]
             /\ turn' = turn
          \/ /\ state[i] = "trying"
             /\ turn = i
             /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "critical"]
             /\ flag' = flag
             /\ turn' = turn
          \/ /\ state[i] = "critical"
             /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "idle"]
             /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![i] = FALSE]
             /\ turn' = turn

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, flag, turn>>

(* Mutual exclusion: no two processes in critical section simultaneously *)
TypeOK == \A i, j \in 1..N: i # j => ~(state[i] = "critical" /\ state[j] = "critical")

====

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A two-phase commit protocol with one coordinator and N participants. The coordinator decides to commit only if all participants vote yes.

Hints: Messages: Prepare, VoteYes, VoteNo, Commit, Abort.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE TwoPhaseCommit ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT Participants
\* Assume each identifier is from {1 .. P} for some constant P
CONSTDEF 
    ParticipantSet == [p \in Participants |-> p]

VARIABLES 
    phase          ,\\ Current phase of the transaction ("init","prepare","commit","abort"), messages, msgs, votes
    votes         ,\\ Mapping from participants to their last received message type or NONE
    messages      ,\\ Multiset of pending messages in transit elements are tuples <sender, receiver, msgType>

(* Message types *)
MessageTypes == {"Prepare", "VoteYes", "VoteNo", "Commit", "Abort"}

(* Helper constants *)
NONE  == <<>> 

(********************************************************************)
*** Type invariant (ensures variables stay within finite domains) ***
(********************************************************************)

TypeOK ==
    /\ phase \in {"init", "prepare", "commit", "abort"}
    /\ votes \in SUBSET[Participants -> MessageTypes]
       (* Each entry maps a participant to one of the defined message types *)
    /\ UNCHANGED @votes[*] \/ (\A v : Votes[v]) #v = NONE => FALSE
    /\ messages \subseteq [{ sender: ANY,
                             receiver: ANY,
                             kind: MessageTypes }]

(********************************************************************
 *** Initial state specification                                 *****
 *******************************************************************)  

Init ==
    /\ phase   = "init"
    /\ votes   = {}
    /\ messages =
        {<"Coordinator", p, "Prepare"> | p \in Participants}

(*********************************************************************
 ***** Next-state relation definition                               *
 * All disjuncts must specify ALL vars either by prime assignment 
 * or using UNCHANGED.                                            *
 *********************************************************************)

Next ==
    LET
      sendMsg(s,r,m) == < s , r , m >

      deliver(msg) ==
          /\ msg.kind \in MessageTypes
          /\ IF msg.receiver = "Coordinator" THEN
                CASE msg.sender \in Participants ->
                    /\ msgs'  = [msgKind IN MessageTypes |
                                  (IF msgKind = "VoteYes" OR msgKind="VoteNo") =>
                                      IF EXISTS x \in participants :
                                         ((x,msgKind)=msg)
                                     THEN [msgs EXCEPT ![msgKind]=TRUE]

                   (* Coordinator updates its vote record *)
              /\\ UNCHANGED <<phase,votes>>
            [] TRUE -> UNCHANGED <<phase,votes>>  

          \/ IF msg.receiver \in Participants THEN
                 /\ IF msg.kind ="Commit" THEN
                        /* Participant commits */
                     /\ UNCHANGED <<votes,messages>>
                  /\ phase'=phase
               /\ votes'=votes
             /\ messages'=UNION(messages,\{sendMsg(msg.receiver,"Coordinator","Ack"\})\}


        in
     /\ (\E p : p \in Participants & NOT p \in DOMAIN(votes)) 
         /\ messageToSend? := {<p,"Coordinator",MessageTypes> | ...}   \\ placeholder for sending Vote requests?

(*********************************************************************
 ***** Termination condition to avoid deadlock                      *
 *********************************************************************)  

Terminating ==
    /\ phase = "commit"
      \/ phase = "abort"

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<phase,messages>>

====

EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N, Participants, Coordinator, PrepareMsg, VoteYesMsg, VoteNoMsg, CommitMsg, AbortMsg, AllMsgs, AllParticipants, AllCoordinators, AllMsgTypes

VARIABLES participants, coordinator, prepareMsg, voteYesMsg, voteNoMsg, commitMsg, abortMsg, allMsgs, allParticipants, allCoordinators, allMsgTypes

Init == /\ participants = Participants
        /\ coordinator = Coordinator
        /\ prepareMsg = PrepareMsg
        /\ voteYesMsg = VoteYesMsg
        /\ voteNoMsg = VoteNoMsg
        /\ commitMsg = CommitMsg
        /\ abortMsg = AbortMsg
        /\ allMsgs = AllMsgs
        /\ allParticipants = AllParticipants
        /\ allCoordinators = AllCoordinators
        /\ allMsgTypes = AllMsgTypes

Next == /\ participants' = participants
        /\ coordinator' = coordinator
        /\ prepareMsg' = prepareMsg
        /\ voteYesMsg' = voteYesMsg
        /\ voteNoMsg' = voteNoMsg
        /\ commitMsg' = commitMsg
        /\ abortMsg' = abortMsg
        /\ allMsgs' = allMsgs
        /\ allParticipants' = allParticipants
        /\ allCoordinators' = allCoordinators
        /\ allMsgTypes' = allMsgTypes

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<participants, coordinator, prepareMsg, voteYesMsg, voteNoMsg, commitMsg, abortMsg, allMsgs, allParticipants, allCoordinators, allMsgTypes>>

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

The dining philosophers problem with N philosophers and N forks. Each philosopher picks up both adjacent forks to eat, then puts them down.

Hints: Track fork ownership and philosopher states: thinking, hungry, eating.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE DiningPhilosophers ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS NumPHILOS

\* States are represented by integers:
STATE_THINKING = 1
STATE_HUNGRY   = 2
_STATE_EATING_ = 3

TypeOK ==
    /\ philState \subseteq [i \in 1 .. NumPHILOS |
          i |-> { STATE_THINKING, STATE_HUNGRY, _STATE_EATING_ }]
    /\ leftForkOwner \subseteq [f \in 1..NumPHILOS |
           f |-> (0 \/ (\E p \in 1..NumPHILOS |-> p))]     \* 0 means no owner
    /\ rightForkOwner \subsete[?]

Init ==  
    /\ UNCHANGED <<leftForkOwner,rightForkOwner>>
    /\ LEFT_FORK_INIT: forall f in 1..NumPhilosophers: leftForkOwner[f]=0
    /\ RIGHT_FORK_INIT: forall f in 1..NumPhil... etc.

Next == ...
====

repaired_spec:
---- MODULE DiningPhilosophers ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N

(* Define philosophers and forks as the set {1, 2, ..., N} *)
Philosophers == 1..N
Forks == 1..N

(* Helper functions to get left and right forks for a philosopher *)
LeftFork(i) == IF i = 1 THEN N ELSE i - 1
RightFork(i) == IF i = N THEN 1 ELSE i + 1

(* State variables *)
VARIABLES state, forks

(* State can be "thinking", "hungry", or "eating" for each philosopher *)
State == {"thinking", "hungry", "eating"}

(* Initial state: all philosophers are thinking and all forks are free *)
Init ==
    /\ state = [p \in Philosophers |-> "thinking"]
    /\ forks = [f \in Forks |-> TRUE]

(* Helper predicate: a philosopher can start eating if both adjacent forks are free *)
CanEat(i) == forks[LeftFork(i)] /\ forks[RightFork(i)]

(* Action: philosopher i picks up both forks and starts eating *)
TakeForks(i) ==
    /\ state[i] = "hungry"
    /\ CanEat(i)
    /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "eating"]
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![LeftFork(i)] = FALSE, ![RightFork(i)] = FALSE]

(* Action: philosopher i puts down both forks and goes back to thinking *)
PutForks(i) ==
    /\ state[i] = "eating"
    /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "thinking"]
    /\ forks' = [forks EXCEPT ![LeftFork(i)] = TRUE, ![RightFork(i)] = TRUE]

(* Action: philosopher i becomes hungry *)
Hungry(i) ==
    /\ state[i] = "thinking"
    /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![i] = "hungry"]
    /\ forks' = forks

(* Next-state relation: any philosopher can transition to the next state *)
Next ==
    \E i \in Philosophers :
        TakeForks(i) \/ PutForks(i) \/ Hungry(i)

(* Invariant: no two adjacent philosophers are eating simultaneously *)
NoAdjacentEating ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        ~(state[i] = "eating" /\ state[IF i = N THEN 1 ELSE i + 1] = "eating")

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only eat if both adjacent forks are free *)
ForksFreeWhenEating ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "eating" => forks[LeftFork(i)] /\ forks[RightFork(i)]

(* Invariant: a fork is held by at most one philosopher *)
ForksHeldByAtMostOne ==
    \A f \in Forks :
        (\A i, j \in Philosophers :
            (f = LeftFork(i) /\ f = LeftFork(j)) => i = j)

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only pick up a fork if it is free *)
ForksFreeBeforePickingUp ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "hungry" => forks[LeftFork(i)] /\ forks[RightFork(i)]

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only put down a fork if it is holding it *)
ForksHeldBeforePuttingDown ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "eating" => ~forks[LeftFork(i)] /\ ~forks[RightFork(i)]

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only become hungry if it is not already hungry *)
NotAlreadyHungry ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "thinking" => ~state[i] = "hungry"

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only become hungry if it is not already eating *)
NotAlreadyEating ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "thinking" => ~state[i] = "eating"

(* Invariant: a philosopher can only become hungry if it is not already thinking *)
NotAlreadyThinking ==
    \A i \in Philosophers :
        state[i] = "thinking" => ~state[i] = "thinking"

(* Specification *)
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, forks>>

(* Type invariant *)
TypeOK ==
    /\ state \in [Philosophers -> State]
    /\ forks \in [Forks -> BOOLEAN]

====

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Lamport's bakery mutual exclusion algorithm for N processes. Processes take a numbered ticket; lower numbers enter first.

Hints: VARIABLES num, flag. Choosing phase before assigning number.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE Bakery ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT n   (* Number of processes *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Variables:
\*    num[p] : current ticket number held by process p (or 0 when not choosing)
\*    flag[p]: boolean indicating whether process p is currently selecting a ticket
VARIABLES num, flag

vars == <<num, flag>>

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
TypeOK ==
        /\ num \in [1 .. n -> Nat]
        /\ flag \in [1 .. n -> BOOLEAN]

Init ==
        /\ num = [p \in 1 .. n |-> 0]
        /\ flag = [p \in 1 .. n |-> FALSE]

Choose(p) ==
        LET maxNum == MAX(num[1..n])
            newTicket == IF maxNum # 0 THEN maxNum + 1 ELSE 1 IN
          /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![p]'=TRUE]
          /\ UNCHANGED[num]
        \/ /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![p]'=FALSE]
           /\ num'[p] = newTicket
           /\ UNCHANGED[[q \in 1 .. n |-> q /= p][num[q]]]

EnterCriticalSection(p) ==
        /\ flag[p] = TRUE
        /\ FORALL r \in 1 .. n :
                (\(r#p => 
                    ((num[r]=0) \/  
                     (IF num[r]<num[p] THEN TRUE

                      (* If equal numbers then lower id wins *) 
                       THEN r < p)))
               )
        /\ UNCHANGED <<flag, num>>

ExitProcess(p) ==
        /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![p]='\*']
        /\ UNCHANGED[<<flag>>]

Next ==
     \E  p \in 1 .. n : Choose(p)
   \/  \A  p \in 1 .. n : EnterCriticalSection(p)
   \/ Terminating

Terminating == /\ UNCHANGED vars

Spec == Init /\ [] Next /\\ TypeOK

====

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A bounded FIFO queue with one producer and one consumer. The producer blocks when the queue is full; the consumer blocks when empty.

Hints: Queue capacity is a constant K.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE BoundedFIFO ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences

CONSTANT K   (* Capacity of the queue *)

VARIABLE q      (* Sequence representing contents *), head, item, q, size, tail
          head    (* Index for next dequeue position *)
          tail    (* Index for next enqueue position *)
          size    (* Current number of elements *)

vars == <<q, head, tail, size>>

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
TypeOk ==
    /\ q \subseteq Nat            (* Elements are natural numbers *)
       /\ Len(q) = size           (* Size matches sequence length *)
       /\ head \in 1 .. K + 1     (* Circular indices in [1,K+1] *)
       /\ tail \in 1 .. K + 1
       /\ size \in 0 .. K         (* Number of items within bounds *)

Init ==
    /\ q = []                     (* Empty queue initially *)
    /\ head = 1                   (* Start at index 1 *)
    /\ tail = 2                    (* Tail points to first free slot (index 2) *)
    /\ size = 0

ProducerAction(item) ==
    LET newTail == IF tail # K THEN tail + 1 ELSE 1 IN
        /\ size < K                 (* Queue not full *)
        /\ item' \notin q             (* Ensure distinctness if desired optional *)
        /\ q' = Append(SeqSubseq(q, 1..size), SeqFromList([item]))
        /\ head' = head               (* Head unchanged when enqueuing *)
        /\ tail' = newTail
        /\ size' = size + 1

ConsumerAction ==
    LET oldHeadVal == SubSequence(q, head-1, head)
          nextIndex   == IF head # K THEN head + 1 ELSE 1 IN
        /\ size > 0                  (* Queue not empty *)
        /\ q' = RemoveAt(head - 1)(q)     (* Shift sequence left by removing front element *)
           \/ UNCHANGED <<head>>       (* If we keep indices fixed: alternative approach below *)

(* Alternative consumer that keeps circular buffer semantics without shifting the whole seq:
   ConsumerCircular ==
      LET consumedItem == ElemAt(q, head)

         in 
            /\ size > 0
            /\ q' = ReplaceNth(q, head, NULL?) *?* (ignored value)
            /\ head' = nextIdx
            /\ tail' = tail
            /\ size' = size - 1

Next ==
    \E item \in Nat :
        ProducerAction(item)

\vee
    ConsumerAction

Spec == Init /\
        [][ Next ]_vars \
        /\ TypeOk

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

The leader election phase of the Raft consensus algorithm. Servers hold terms and vote for candidates. A candidate wins if it gets a majority of votes.

Hints: States: Follower, Candidate, Leader. VARIABLES currentTerm, votedFor, state.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE RaftLeaderElection ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets, Integers

CONSTANT ServerSet
\* For simplicity we assume server identifiers are natural numbers from 1 up to MaxServerId
CONSTDEF MAXSERVERID : Nat  

ASSUME ServerSet = {s | s \in 1 .. MAXSERVERID}

VARIABLES votedFor, state, TLC, currentTerm
StateVal == {"Follower","Candidate","Leader"}

TypeOK ==
    /\ currentTerm \in Int          (* bounded by TLC's finite range assumption on INT *)
    /\ (votedFor \in ServerSet \/ votedFor = NULL)
    /\ state \in StateVal

Init ==
    /\ TypeOK
    /\ currentTerm = -1              \* initial term before any elections start
    /\ votedFor = NULL               \* no one has been voted yet
    /\ state = "Follower"

VoteRequest(cand) ==
    LET newTerm == cand.term IN

            /\ currentTerm' = newTerm
            /\ votedFor' = cand.id
            /\ UNCHANGED vars[3]      \* keep state unchanged for this simple model
        ELSEIF newTerm < currentTerm THEN
            /\ UNCHANGED vars           \* reject vote: nothing changes


                /\ UNCHANGED vars     \* cannot grant another vote in same term

                /\ votedFor' = cand.id
                /\ UNCHANGED <<currentTerm, state>>

CandidateStart(id) ==
    /\ state = "Follower"
    /\ currentTerm' = currentTerm + 1
    /\ votedFor' = id          \* self-vote
    /\ state' = "Candidate" 

LeaderCommit ==
    /\ state = "Candidate"
    /\ majorityVotesReceived()
    /\ state' = "Leader"
    /\ UNCHANGED <<votedFor,currentTerm>>  

majorityVotesReceived() ==
    (* In the simplified spec we assume an oracle function that returns TRUE when enough votes are received.
       For TLC to check properties it must be bounded. We use a nondeterministic choice over {TRUE,FALSE}. *)
    CHOOSE b : (b \in BOOLEAN)

Next ==  
    \/ CandidateStart(ANY)
    \/ VoteRequest(CANDIDATE_MSG)
    \/ LeaderCommit

(* Dummy message structures for modeling purposes *)

CANDIDATE_MSG == [id: ServerSet term: Int]
ANY == ANY

Spec == Init /\ []_(vars)[[ Next ]]

---- MODULE RaftLeaderElection ----
EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, TLC
CONSTANTS NULL

VARIABLES currentTerm, votedFor, state

(* --type definitions-- *)
StateType == {"Follower", "Candidate", "Leader"}
ServerId == 1..3

(* --initialization-- *)
Init == /\ currentTerm = 0
        /\ votedFor = [s \in ServerId |-> NULL]
        /\ state = [s \in ServerId |-> "Follower"]

(* --state transitions-- *)
FollowerToCandidate(s) == /\ state[s] = "Follower"
                         /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![s] = "Candidate"]
                         /\ currentTerm' = currentTerm + 1
                         /\ votedFor' = [votedFor EXCEPT ![s] = s]
                         /\ UNCHANGED <<currentTerm, votedFor, state>>

CandidateToLeader(s) == /\ state[s] = "Candidate"
                       /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![s] = "Leader"]
                       /\ UNCHANGED <<currentTerm, votedFor, state>>

(* --vote request handling-- *)
VoteRequest(s, t, v) == /\ state[s] = "Follower"
                        /\ t >= currentTerm
                        /\ (votedFor[s] = NULL \/ votedFor[s] = v)
                        /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![s] = "Follower"]
                        /\ currentTerm' = t
                        /\ votedFor' = [votedFor EXCEPT ![s] = v]
                        /\ UNCHANGED <<state>>

(* --next state-- *)
Next == \E s \in ServerId :
          FollowerToCandidate(s)
        \/ CandidateToLeader(s)
        \/ VoteRequest(s, currentTerm, NULL)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<currentTerm, votedFor, state>>

(* --type invariant-- *)
TypeOK == /\ currentTerm \in Nat
          /\ votedFor \in [ServerId -> (ServerId \cup {NULL})]
          /\ state \in [ServerId -> StateType]

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A read-write lock allowing multiple concurrent readers but exclusive writer access. Writers wait for all readers to finish.

Hints: VARIABLES readers, writer_active.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE ReadWriteLock ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences

CONSTANT MAX_READERS   (* maximum number of simultaneous readers *)

VARIABLES readers, writerActive

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Readers count; integer between 0 .. MAX_READERS inclusive
TypeOk ==
    readerCountInRange == 
        (\E r : 1 <= r <= MAX_READERS) \/ TRUE  -- placeholder for range check
    writersAreBoolean == writerActive \in BOOLEAN 

vars == <<readers, writerActive>>

Init ==
    /\ readers = 0
    /\ writerActive = FALSE

Next ==
    LET nextReaders == IF writerActive THEN readers ELSE readers' IN
    CASE
      | /* Reader requests entry */
          /\ ~writerActive
          /\ (readers < MAX_READERS)
          /\ readers' = readers + 1
          /\ writerActive' = writerActive

       []/* Writer attempts acquisition when no active writer and zero readers*/
          /\ (~writerActive)
          /\ (readers = 0)
          /\ writerActive' = TRUE
          /\ UNCHANGED [reader := reader]

     []/* Writer releases lock after completion */
         /\ writerActive
         /\ writerActive' = FALSE
         /\ UNCHANGED [readers]

     []/* Reader exits the critical section */
         /\ (readers > 0)
         /\ readers' = readers - 1
         /\ UNCHANGED [writerActive] 

     [] Terminating   (* allow system to terminate *)
           /\ UNCHANGED vars

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

repaired_spec:
---- MODULE ReadWriteLock ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N \* Number of readers

VARIABLES readers, writer_active

(* readers is a set of reader IDs currently holding the lock *)
(* writer_active is a boolean: 1 if a writer holds the lock, 0 otherwise *)

Init == /\ readers = {}
        /\ writer_active = 0

(* Reader i attempts to acquire the lock *)
ReaderAcquire(i) == 
    /\ i \in 1..N
    /\ writer_active = 0
    /\ readers' = readers \cup {i}
    /\ UNCHANGED writer_active

(* Reader i releases the lock *)
ReaderRelease(i) == 
    /\ i \in readers
    /\ readers' = readers \ {i}
    /\ UNCHANGED writer_active

(* Writer attempts to acquire the lock *)
WriterAcquire == 
    /\ writer_active = 0
    /\ readers = {}
    /\ writer_active' = 1
    /\ UNCHANGED readers

(* Writer releases the lock *)
WriterRelease == 
    /\ writer_active = 1
    /\ writer_active' = 0
    /\ UNCHANGED readers

Next == \/ \E i \in 1..N: ReaderAcquire(i)
        \/ \E i \in readers: ReaderRelease(i)
        \/ WriterAcquire
        \/ WriterRelease

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<readers, writer_active>>

(* Invariants *)
TypeOK == /\ readers \subseteq 1..N
          /\ writer_active \in {0,1}
          /\ (writer_active = 1 => readers = {})

NoConcurrentWriters == \A i, j \in 1..N: ~(i # j /\ i \in readers /\ j \in readers)

NoReaderDuringWrite == \A i \in 1..N: ~(writer_active = 1 /\ i \in readers)

====

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

The Chandy-Lamport distributed snapshot algorithm over a network of N processes with FIFO channels. Each process records its local state and the state of incoming channels.

Hints: Send marker messages along each outgoing channel after recording state.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE DistributedSnapshot ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT P, OutNeighbors
VARIABLES procState, recChanMsgs, recorder, chanMsg


(* Types *)
TypeInvariant ==
    /\ procState : P -> {"unrecorded","recorder"}
    /\ chanMsg : (OutChannels x InChannels) -> Sequence[Message]
    (* For simplicity we assume Message is just an atom no type needed *)

Init ==
    /\ \A p \in P :
          \/ procState[p] = "unrecorded"
            /\ UNCHANGED <<chanMsg>>
    /\ recorder = NULL
    /\ recChanMsgs = {}

Next == 
    LET nextProc(p) == IF procState[p]="unrecorded" THEN
                        /* process starts a new snapshot */
                          /\ recorder' = p
                          /\ procState'[p] = "recorder"
                      ELSEIF procState[p]="recorder" AND recorder'=NULL THEN
                            /* recording finished for this process: send markers to all outgoing neighbors*/
                              /\ SEND_MARKERS_TO_OUTGOING(p)

    in  /\ (\E p \in P : nextProc(p))
      /\ UNCHANGED <<procState, recorder>>   // other variables unchanged unless explicitly updated below

SEND_MARKERS_TO_OUTGOING(proc) ==
    /\ \A q \in OutNeighbors(proc):
           chanMsg'[(proc,q)] = Append(chanMsg[(proc,q)], Marker)
       /\ UNCHANGED [x |-> y IN {proc} UNION OUTCHANNELS  \union  RECCHANMS]

(* Termination *)
Terminating ==
     /\ recorder = NULL
     /\ UNCHANGED <<procState, chanMsg>>

Spec == Init /\\ [] ( Next \/ Terminating ) 

====

---- MODULE ChandyLamport ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences

CONSTANT N
ASSUME N \in 1..10

VARIABLES state, channel, marker

(* Type constraints *)
TypeOK == /\ state \in [1..N -> {"active", "recorded"}]
          /\ channel \in [1..N -> [1..N -> {"empty", "msg"}]]
          /\ marker \in [1..N -> [1..N -> BOOLEAN]]

(* Initial state: all processes active, channels empty, no markers *)
Init == /\ state = [i \in 1..N |-> "active"]
        /\ channel = [i \in 1..N |-> [j \in 1..N |-> "empty"]]
        /\ marker = [i \in 1..N |-> [j \in 1..N |-> FALSE]]

(* Process i records its state and sends markers on all outgoing channels *)
Record(i) == /\ state[i] = "recorded"
             /\ \A j \in 1..N : marker[i][j] = TRUE

(* Sending a marker on channel i->j *)
SendMarker(i, j) == /\ channel[i][j] = "empty"
                   /\ channel' = [channel EXCEPT ![i][j] = "msg"]
                   /\ marker' = [marker EXCEPT ![i][j] = FALSE]
                   /\ UNCHANGED <<state>>

(* Receiving a marker on channel j->i *)
ReceiveMarker(j, i) == /\ channel[j][i] = "msg"
                      /\ channel' = [channel EXCEPT ![j][i] = "empty"]
                      /\ marker' = [marker EXCEPT ![j][i] = FALSE]
                      /\ UNCHANGED <<state>>

(* Sending a normal message on channel i->j *)
SendMsg(i, j) == /\ channel[i][j] = "empty"
                 /\ channel' = [channel EXCEPT ![i][j] = "msg"]
                 /\ UNCHANGED <<state, marker>>

(* Receiving a normal message on channel j->i *)
ReceiveMsg(j, i) == /\ channel[j][i] = "msg"
                   /\ channel' = [channel EXCEPT ![j][i] = "empty"]
                   /\ UNCHANGED <<state, marker>>

(* Next-state relation *)
Next == \E i \in 1..N :
          \E j \in 1..N :
            (state[i] = "active" /\ Record(i) /\ SendMarker(i, j))
            \/ (marker[j][i] /\ ReceiveMarker(j, i))
            \/ (SendMsg(i, j))
            \/ (ReceiveMsg(j, i))

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<state, channel, marker>>

====

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A token ring network with N nodes. Each node passes a single token clockwise. Only the token holder may send a message.

Hints: Nodes 1..N, token position as a variable.

---- MODULE TokenRing ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets, Integers

CONSTANT N   (* number of nodes *)

VARIABLE tpos      (* current owner of the token integer from 1 .. N *), msg, tpos
                      NIL means no pending message *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Constants for convenience
TokenPositions == {i : i \in 1..N}
MsgValues     == {"msg" , @}    \* '@' denotes nil \div empty message

TypeOk ==
        /\ tpos \in TokenPositions
        /\ msg \in MsgValues

Init ==
        /\ tpos = 1          \* start at node 1
        /\ msg = @           \* initially nothing is transmitted

Next ==
        \/ (tpos < N) => 
            /\ tpos' = tpos + 1
            /\ IF msg # @ THEN UNCHANGED msg ELSE msg' = @
               * If there was an outgoing message it stays until delivered.
        \/ (tpos >= N) =>
            /\ tpos' = 1
            /\ IF msg # @ THEN UNCHANGED msg ELSE msg' = @
        \/ (\E m \in MsgValues :
                /\ msg = @         \* only when idle can send a new one
                /\ msg' = m)
        \/ Terminating   \* allow deadlock if desired: system may stop.

Terminating ==
        /\ UNCHANGED <<tpos, msg>>

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<tpos,msg>> 

====

---- MODULE TokenRing ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N
ASSUME N \in Nat /\ N > 0

VARIABLES token, sent

(* token: 1..N, the node currently holding the token
   sent: set of nodes that have sent a message in the current round *)

(* Helper to compute the next node in the ring *)
NextNode(i) == IF i = N THEN 1 ELSE i + 1

(* Initial state: node 1 holds the token, no messages sent *)
Init == token = 1 /\ sent = {}

(* Action: token passes to the next node *)
Pass == token' = NextNode(token) /\ sent' = sent

(* Action: current token holder sends a message *)
Send == token' = token /\ sent' = sent \cup {token}

(* Next-state relation: either pass the token or send a message *)
Next == Pass \/ Send

(* Fairness: the token circulates infinitely often *)
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<token, sent>>

(* Invariants *)
TypeOK == /\ token \in 1..N
          /\ sent \subseteq 1..N

\* Next disjunction:

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Single-decree Paxos consensus over N acceptors and M proposers. Once a value is chosen, it is never changed.

Hints: Phases: Prepare/Promise, Accept/Accepted. Proposer picks value with highest ballot.

---- MODULE SingleDecreePaxos ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets, Sequences

CONSTANT NumAcceptors, NumProposers, ACCEPTOR_IDS, BALLLOT_NUMBERS, PROPOSER_IDS
    /* Bound for ballots so they are enumerable */
    BallotBound > 0 

VARIABLES 
    pBallots      ,  (* mapping from each proposer id -> current proposal number *), accAcks, accPromises, pValues, proposer
    pValues       ,  (* proposed values by each proposer may change until accepted *)

    accPromises   ,  (* map[acceptorId] : [ballot |-> {maxPromised} ]*)
    accAcks        ,  (* map[accid][proposalNumber]->{value,votedByMaxB} *)

(* Helper constants *)
PROPOSER_IDS \in 1..NumProposers
ACCEPTOR_IDS \in 1..NumAcceptors
BALLOT_NUMBERS == 1 .. BallotBound

(********************************************************************)
(** Type invariants **)

TypeOK ==
    /\ pBallots \subseteq PROPOSER_IDS --> BALLOT_NUMBERS
    /\ pValues \subseteq PROPOSER_IDS --> {"v" }          // placeholder value set

    /\ accPromises \subseteq ACCEPTOR_IDS --> (BALLOT_NUMBERS --> BOOLEAN)   
         \/ (\a IN ACCEPTOR_IDS: accPromises[a] = <<>>)     // no promise yet

    /\ accAcks \subseteq ACCEPTOR_IDS -->
           ((\b IN BALLLOT_NUMBERS): ({}) )                 // empty ack sets initially

(********************************************************************
** Initial state **
*)

Init ==
    /\ pBallots' = [p \in PROPOSER_IDS |->
            CHOOSE b \in BALLLOT_NUMBERS WITH TRUE]
       /* each proposer starts with some ballot number */

    /\ pValues' = [p \in PROPOSER_IDS |-> "initVal"]   (* arbitrary initial values *)

    /\ accPromises' = [ a \in ACCEPTOR_IDS |
                        IF FALSE THEN [] ELSE 
                           [b \in BALLLOT_NUMBERS |\> FALSE]]  
        /* all promises false at start */

    /\ accAcks'  = [ a \in ACCEPTOR_IDS |
                     [b \in BALLLOT_NUMBERS |>\ {} ] ]


(* Helper predicates *)

HighestPromised(acc, ballotsSet) == 
    LET maxB == MAX { b : b \in ballotsSet } 
    IN
      /\ EXISTS v,votedByMax :
          (accAcks[acc][maxB]) = <v , votedByMax>

PreparePhase(pid,pballot,val) ==
    /\ pBallots[p] <= pballot                (* proposer's current proposal no less than new one? *)
    /\ UNCHANGED <<pValues>>
    /\ (\a \in ACCEPTOR_IDS:
           CASE 
             /\\ accPromises[a][pballot] => * // already promised for this or higher?
                 UNCHANGED <<
                      accPromises,
                      accAcks >>

            \/ TRUE =>
              /\ accPromises'[a][pballot] := TRUE
              /\ accAcks'[a][pballot]   := {}
               )

AcceptPhase(pid,bval) ==
    /\ UNCHANGED <<pBallots>>   
    /\ (\a \in ACCEPTOR_IDS:  

            /\ accAcks'  [a][pBallots[pid]]
                  := <bval,{ pid }>

            UNCHANGED <<accPromises,accAcks>>

(* Next state *)

Next ==

(*
Proposer initiates a Prepare with its ballot number and value.
*)
\* Disjuncts must cover all variables; we use prime notation.

\/ (/* Propose action */)
      LET pid IN PROPOSER_IDS :
          PREPARE_ACTION(pId,pBallots[p], pValues[p])  

/\ (* Accept phase after promises *)
     LET pid IN PROPOSER_IDS : 
       ACCEPT_PHASE(pid)

---- MODULE SingleDecreePaxos ----
EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, TLC

CONSTANTS N, M, V

VARIABLES acceptors, proposers, promises, accepted, chosen

(* Types *)
TypeOK == 
  /\ acceptors \in [1..N -> [ballot: 1..N, value: V]]
  /\ proposers \in [1..M -> [ballot: 1..N, value: V]]
  /\ promises \in [1..N -> [ballot: 1..N, value: V]]
  /\ accepted \in [1..N -> [ballot: 1..N, value: V]]
  /\ chosen \in [1..N -> [ballot: 1..N, value: V]]

(* Initial state *)
Init == 
  /\ acceptors = [i \in 1..N |-> [ballot |-> 0, value |-> <<>>]]
  /\ proposers = [j \in 1..M |-> [ballot |-> 0, value |-> <<>>]]
  /\ promises = [i \in 1..N |-> [ballot |-> 0, value |-> <<>>]]
  /\ accepted = [i \in 1..N |-> [ballot |-> 0, value |-> <<>>]]
  /\ chosen = [i \in 1..N |-> [ballot |-> 0, value |-> <<>>]]

(* Prepare/Promise phase *)
Prepare(j) == 
  /\ proposers' = [proposers EXCEPT ![j].ballot = proposers[j].ballot + 1,
                                   ![j].value = proposers[j].value]
  /\ acceptors' = acceptors
  /\ promises' = [i \in 1..N |-> 
          [ballot |-> proposers[j].ballot + 1, value |-> proposers[j].value]]
  /\ accepted' = accepted
  /\ chosen' = chosen

(* Accept/Accepted phase *)
Accept(j) == 
  /\ proposers' = proposers
  /\ acceptors' = acceptors
  /\ promises' = promises
  /\ accepted' = [i \in 1..N |-> 
          [ballot |-> proposers[j].ballot, value |-> proposers[j].value]]
  /\ chosen' = chosen

(* Chosen phase *)
Chosen(j) == 
  /\ proposers' = proposers
  /\ acceptors' = acceptors
  /\ promises' = promises
  /\ accepted' = accepted
  /\ chosen' = [i \in 1..N |-> 
          [ballot |-> proposers[j].ballot, value |-> proposers[j].value]]

(* Next state *)
Next == 
  \/ \E j \in 1..M : Prepare(j)
  \/ \E j \in 1..M : Accept(j)
  \/ \E j \in 1..M : Chosen(j)

(* Spec *)
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<acceptors, proposers, promises, accepted, chosen>> /\ TypeOK

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A sender transmits a file in chunks over an unreliable channel. The sender retransmits up to MAX_RETRIES times before giving up.

Hints: Model message loss as non-deterministic. Track retransmit count.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE FileTransfer ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets, Integers

CONSTANT CHUNK_SIZE, FILE_LENGTH, PACKET_ID, PACKET_IDS

    /* Maximum number of retries for each chunk */
    MAX_RETRY     >= 1,

    /* Set of all possible packet identifiers (chunk indices) */
    PACKET_IDS      : SUBSET Nat,

    /* Channel capacity is unlimited but we model it explicitly */
    CHANNEL_CAPACITY : Nat 

/* Derived constants */
VARIABLES 
    sentChunks ,          (* Sequence of packets already transmitted *), ackedPackets, channelBuffer, currentChunkIndex, retryCount
    ackedPackets ,        (* Subset of PACKET_IDS acknowledged by receiver *)
    retryCount ,         (* Mapping from PACKET_ID -> Int representing remaining attempts *)
    currentChunkIndex ,  (* Index into sequence of data being sent *)
    channelBuffer        (* Queue modeling the unreliably transmitting channel *)

(* Type constraints and invariants *)
TypeOK == /\ sentChunks \in Seq(PACKET_IDS)
           /\ ackedPackets \subseteq PACKET_IDS
           /\ UNCHANGED <<sentChunks>>
           /\ forall p \in PACKET_IDS:
                (\E r: r = retryCount[p] \/ r # retryCount[p])  
               /\ retryCount[p] \in 0..MAX_RETRY + 1
           /\ currentChunkIndex \in 0 .. Len(sentChunks)+CHANNEL_CAPACITY
           /\ channelBuffer \in Seq(Seq(Nat))

Init ==
/* Initially no chunks are in flight, nothing has been received,
   each chunk can be retried MAX_RETRY times. The sender starts with first packet index zero.
*/
\land sentChunks = []
\land ackedPackets = {}
\and retryCount = [p |-> (IF p <= FILE_LENGTH THEN MAX_RETRY ELSE -1)]
\land currentChunkIndex = 0 
\land channelBuffer = []

SendPacket(p) ==
/\ p \notin sentChunks
\/ p > LEN(channelBuffer)
/\
channelBuffer' = Append(channelBuffer , [[p]])
retryCount'[p] = MAX_RETRY

AckReceived(p) ==
/*
 * Non-deterministic message loss is modeled by allowing the receiver to either deliver or drop an ACK.
 */
UNLESS p \# IN CHANNEL_BUFFER then skip else (
    /* Receiver acknowledges and removes from buffer if present */
    UNCHANGED <<sentChunks>>
    /\ ackedPackets' = Insert(ackedPackets,p)
    /\ channelBuffer' = RemoveFirstWhere(\x: x[1]=p)(channelBuffer)

)

/*\* Retransmission logic for a single packet **/
RetransmitIfNeeded(p) ==  
/* If we have not yet exceeded retries, send again otherwise give up on this packet */   
((retryCount[p]>0) => SendPacket(p))
 \/ ((retryCount[p]<=0) =>
     (* Give up – no further action needed. *)
      UnchangedVars )

UnchangedVars == /\ sentChunks'=sentChunks
                 /\ retryCount'=retryCount
                 /\ channelBuffer'=channelBuffer

Next ==
(* The sender can choose one of several actions:
   1. Transmit next chunk (if any remain).
   2. Process acknowledgment received in channel buffer.
   3. Retry sending packets that are still pending but lost.
*)
\/ 
/\ currentChunkIndex < FILE_LENGTH          // there remains data to be transmitted
/\
SendPacket(currentChunkIndex+1)
/\ currentChunkIndex' = currentChunkIndex + 1

\\/ 
/\ Len(channelBuffer)>0                      /* There is at least an ACK waiting */
\\ AckReceived([first |-> first][currentChannel] ) 

\\/ 
FORALL p \in PACKET_IDS :
    (\E r : r=retryCount[p]) -> (
        IF retryCount[p]>0 THEN RetransmitIfNeeded(p)

       )

/* Termination: if all chunks have been acknowledged or given up, we stop transmitting */  
Terminating ==
UNLESS ackedPackets # PACKET_IDS \/ EXISTS p:\(p <=FILE_LENGTH) & retryCount[p]<=-1 then skip else UnchangedVars  

Spec == Init /\\ [][ Next ]_<< sentChunks ,ackdPackets ,retryCount ,
                               currentChunkIndex ,channelBuffer >> /\ TypeOK

====

---- MODULE FileTransfer ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N, MAX_RETRIES

VARIABLES seq, ack, retries, state

TypeOK == /\ seq \in 0..N
          /\ ack \in 0..N
          /\ retries \in 0..MAX_RETRIES
          /\ state \in {"idle", "sending", "waiting_ack", "done", "failed"}

Init == /\ seq = 0
        /\ ack = 0
        /\ retries = 0
        /\ state = "idle"

SendChunk == /\ state = "idle"
             /\ seq < N
             /\ state' = "sending"

Transmit == /\ state = "sending"
            /\ state' = "waiting_ack"

AckReceived == /\ state = "waiting_ack"
               /\ ack = seq
               /\ state' = "idle"
               /\ seq' = seq + 1
               /\ retries' = 0

AckLost == /\ state = "waiting_ack"
           /\ ack # seq
           /\ state' = "waiting_ack"
           /\ retries' = retries + 1
           /\ retries' <= MAX_RETRIES

GiveUp == /\ state = "waiting_ack"
          /\ retries = MAX_RETRIES
          /\ state' = "failed"

Done == /\ seq = N
        /\ state' = "done"

Next == \E s \in {"idle", "sending", "waiting_ack", "done", "failed"}:
          /\ state' = s
          /\ UNCHANGED <<seq, ack, retries>>

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<seq, ack, retries, state>>

====

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A database with snapshot isolation. Transactions read a consistent snapshot. Write-write conflicts cause an abort.

Hints: Model transactions as spanning multiple steps with begin/commit/abort.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE SnapshotIsolation ----

EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences, FiniteSets, TLC, Integers

CONSTANT DBKeys, Key, SEQ, Val
VARIABLES tid, transaction, txs


(* Types *)

TypeDBKey      == [key : DBKeys]
TypeValue       == Nat        (* For simplicity values are natural numbers *)
TypeVersion     == Nat         (* Version number for each key in the database *)

\* Database is mapping from Key -> Value and version
dbRecord(key) ==
    <<value |-> dbState[key].val,
      ver   |-> dbState[key].ver>>

TxId           == Nat            (* Transaction identifiers *)
TxStatus       == {“running”, “committed”, “aborted”}
ReadSetEntry   == [txid  : TxId ,
                   key   : DBKeys ,
                   val   : TypeValue ,
                   ver   : TypeVersion ]
WriteSetEntry  == [txid  : TxId ,
                    key   : DBKeys ,
                    newVal: TypeValue ]

TransactionRec =
[ id      : TxId 
  status  : TxStatus 
  readset : Seq(ReadSetEntry)
  writeset: Seq(WriteSetEntry)
]

InitDb(dbVals) ==
    /\ \A k \in DBKeys :
        (k \mapsto <<val|->dbVals[k], 
                     ver|->[0]>>) \subseteq dbState

InitTx(txsList, nextID) ==
    txs = tlist2seq(txsList)

(* Helper to convert a set of transactions into sequence preserving order by ID *)
tlist2seq(ts) ==

         LET minT == CHOOSE tid IN DOMAIN ts:
                      MIN (\{tid' \mid tid'\in DOMAIN ts\})
          IN <<<ts[minT]]> , tlist2seq(\{i | i in DOMAIN ts & i /= minT})>


TypeOK ==
    /\ dbState \in SUBSET(DBKeys -> [value: TypeValue,
                                     ver  : TypeVersion])
    /\ txs     \in SEQ(TransactionRec)

Next ==
    \/ BeginStep
    \/ ReadStep
    \/ WriteStep
    \/ CommitAttempt
    \/ AbortStep
    \/ Terminating

BeginStep ==
    /* Start new transaction with unique id */
    let maxId == MAX({tx.id | tx <- txs} \cup {1-1})   (* if no tx yet use -1? but we need >=0 so start at 0*)
        newId == (IF maxId=-1 THEN 0 ELSE maxId+1)
      IN 
           /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState, txs>>
           /\ txs = Append(txs, 
               <<id->newId ,
                 status->"running",
                 readset->[],
                 writeset->[ ]>> )

ReadStep ==
    LET txn == CHOOSE t \IN txs :
                t.status="running"
            keyToRead == CHOICE k \in DBKeys:
                            TRUE          (* nondeterministically pick a key *)
         IN
             /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState, txs[txn.index].writeset >>
             /\ dbRecord(keyToRead) != NULL
             /\ txs' =
                  [t in DOMAIN(txns):

                          <| txn |
                             readset -> Append(
                                 txn.readset,
                                 <<txid->txn.id ,key->keyToRead,
                                   val->dbState[keyToRead].val,
                                   ver->dbState[keyToRead].ver>>)|
                         |>


WriteStep ==
    /* Transaction adds an entry to its write set */
    let txn == CHOOSE t\IN txs : t.status="running"
        targetKey   == CHOICE k \in DBKeys:TRUE
        newValue     == CHOICE v \in 0..10:TRUE      (* bounded value space for simplicity *)
       IN 
           /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState>>
           /\ txs = [
               i \in DOMAIN(txs):  

                     <| txn |
                        writeset ->
                           Append( txn.writeset ,
                               <<txid->txn.id,key->targetKey,newVal->newValue>> ) |>   


CommitAttempt ==
    LET committingTx == CHOOSE t \IN txs :
                            t.status ="running"         

         conflict? == EXISTS w1,w2 \in SEQ(committingTx.writeset) :
                      (w1.key=w2.key & w1.txid /= w2.txid)

         -- For each key in the transaction's readset, check if version changed since snapshot.
         stale?(k,v,rVer) ==
             dbState[k].ver > rVer 

         stalenessExists ==
            EXISTS rsEntry \in Seq(committingTx.readset):
                (\E kv,\vr:\exists e\in Seq(dbRecord(rsEntry.key)):
                    FALSE)
              /* placeholder actual logic below */

         validReadSet ==
            ~stale?

        IN
           \/  /\ !conflict?
                  /\ !validReadSet   (* no conflicts and reads are still consistent *)
                  /\ UNCHANGED<<dbState>>
                  /\ txs' = [i \in DOMAIN(txs) |-> IF i=commitingTx.index THEN <| commitingTx | status -> "committeed">>]

           \/  /\ TRUE      (* abort on any failure: write-write or stale read set *)
               /\ txs'= [
                   i \in DOMAIN(txns):

                         <| txn |
                            status->"aborted"|>


AbortStep ==  
    /\ UNCHANGED <<dbState>>   
    /\ txs' =
          [t \IN DOMAIN(txs):
                 IF t.status="running"
                      THEN <| t |status->"aborted">


Terminating ==
     /\ UNCHANGED dbState,txs

Spec == InitDb(DBKeys) /\
       [][][Next]_vars 

====

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

N nodes exchange clock values to synchronise. After one round, all clocks are within epsilon of each other.

Hints: Drift modelled as integer offset. Average-based sync.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE ClockSync ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets, Integers

CONSTANT NumNodes, MaxOffset
VARIABLES clocks, offsets


(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)    
\* Types and invariants -------------------------------------------------

TypeInvariant ==
    /\ len(offsets) = NumNodes
    /\ len(clocks) = NumNodes
    /\ (\A i \in DOMAIN offsets : -MaxOffset <= offsets[i] <= MaxOffset)
    /\ (\A j \i n DOMAIN clocks : clocks[j] >= 0)

(\* All variables must be finite sets; we restrict them accordingly.\*)

vars == <<offsets, clocks>>

Init ==
    /\ TypeInvariant
    /\ offsets' = [k \in 1..NumNodes |-> 0]
    /\ clocks'  = [j \in 1..NumNodes |-> 0]

Next ==
    \/ StepDrift            (* apply random bounded drift to all nodes *)
    \/ SyncRound             (* perform a synchronization round *)
    \/ Terminating           (* allow termination if desired *)

StepDrift ==
        LET newOffsets == [n \in 1..NumNodes |

                                offsets[n] + RandomChoice(-MaxOffset .. +MaxOffset)

                               offsets[n]]
         IN  
          /\ UNCHANGED clocks
          /\ offsets' = newOffsets

SyncRound ==
      LET avgClock   == (SUM_{c \in DOMAIN clocks} clocks[c]) \div Len(DOMAIN clocks)
          delta       == { c \in DOMAIN clocks :

                              Sign(avgClock - clocks[c])

                             0 }
          updatedClks == [i \in DOMAIN clocks |-> clocks[i]+delta(i)]
     IN
          /\ clocks' = updatedClks
          /\ UNCHANGEd offsets

Terminating ==
    /\ UNCHANGED vars

Spec == Init /\ []<>(Next)

====

EXTENDS Integers, Sequences

CONSTANTS N, epsilon

VARIABLES clocks, offsets

TypeOK == /\ clocks \in [1..N -> Int]
          /\ offsets \in [1..N -> Int]

Init == /\ clocks = [i \in 1..N |-> 0]
        /\ offsets = [i \in 1..N |-> 0]

Next ==
  /\ \E i \in 1..N :
       /\ clocks' = [clocks EXCEPT ![i] = clocks[i] + offsets[i]]
       /\ offsets' = offsets
  \/ \E i, j \in 1..N :
       /\ offsets' = [offsets EXCEPT ![i] = offsets[i] + (clocks[j] - clocks[i]) \div 2,
                                      ![j] = offsets[j] + (clocks[i] - clocks[j]) \div 2]
       /\ clocks' = clocks

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

Peterson's mutual exclusion algorithm for exactly 2 processes.

---- MODULE Peterson ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT n \doteq 2   (* number of processes *)

VARIABLES flags, turn

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Flags are booleans indicating intent to enter critical section
flags \in [1 .. n -> BOOLEAN]

turn \in {1 , 2}

Init ==
    /\ flags = [i \in 1 .. n |-> FALSE]
    /\ turn = 1

Next ==
    \/ Process(1)
     \/ Process(2)

    LET j \be i # 1 + 1 IN        \* other process index *

      /\ flags[i] := TRUE          \
         /\ turn' := j             \
         /\ UNCHANGED <<flags[j]>>
       \/ * Wait until it is not my turn or opponent leaves*
           (turn#=i => ~flags[j]) 
            /\ UNCHANGED <<flags>>
       \/ * Enter CS and leave immediately after execution*
           /\ UNCHANGED <<flags>>  
           /\ UNCHANGED <<turn>>

Spec == Init /\
        [][][ Next ]_<<flags, turn>>

TypeOK ==
    /\ flags \subseteq DOMAIN[BOOLEAN]
    /\ turn \in {1 , 2}

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A gossip (epidemic) protocol where N nodes periodically share updates. A node that receives an update marks it as 'infected' and spreads it in subsequent rounds.

Hints: VARIABLES known, infected sets. Eventual convergence is a liveness property.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE GossipProtocol ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS NodeSet, Node
VARIABLES infection


(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Initial condition:
Init ==
    /\ infection = [n \in NodeSet |-> {}]   (* no one knows anyone yet *)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Each round consists of two phases for each node:
\* Phase 1 – send phase: choose another node uniformly at random,
\*                then spread your entire set of infections to them;
\* Phase 2 – receive phase: merge received information into own state.
Next ==
    \/ (\E sender \in NodeSet :
            (\E receiver \in NodeSet : receiver # sender ) ->
              LET recvInfo == infection[sender] IN
                  /\ infection' =
                        [ n \in NodeSet |
                            IF n = receiver THEN (recvInfo  \union  infection[n])

                    UNCHANGED <<>>)
      \/ Terminating                     (* allow termination if desired *)

Terminating ==
    /\ UNCHANGED vars                 (* system may stop without changing anything *)

Spec == Init /\ []<>(Next)     (* liveness is expressed separately as a temporal property, not in Spec itself *)

TypeOK ==
    /\ NodeSet \subseteq Nat 
       /\ NodeSet /= {}
    /\ infection \in [Node -> SUBSET Node]
        /\ DOMAIN infection = NodeSet

---- MODULE Gossip ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANTS N, Nodes

(* Each node is identified by an integer in 1..N *)
NodeSet == 1..N

(* State: for each node, a set of messages it knows *)
VARIABLES known

(* Initial state: no node knows any message *)
Init == known = [i \in NodeSet |-> {}]

(* Helper: a node i chooses a random neighbor j (j != i) *)
ChooseNeighbor(i) == CHOOSE j \in NodeSet : j # i

(* Helper: node i sends all its known messages to neighbor j *)
Send(i, j) == 
  LET msgs == known[i] IN
  /\ known' = [k \in NodeSet |-> IF k = j THEN known[k] \cup msgs ELSE known[k]]

(* One round of gossip: each node picks a neighbor and sends its knowledge *)
Round == 
  \E i \in NodeSet :
    LET j == ChooseNeighbor(i) IN
    Send(i, j)

(* Next-state relation: one round of gossip *)
Next == Round

(* Spec: execution of the protocol *)
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_known

(* Invariant: all known sets are subsets of the universe of messages *)
TypeOK == \A i \in NodeSet : known[i] \subseteq 1..N

====

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A memory allocator managing a fixed pool of N pages. Clients request and release pages. Safety: no page is allocated to two clients simultaneously.

Hints: VARIABLES free, allocated. allocated is a function from Client to SUBSET Pages.

---- MODULE MemoryAllocator ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N, Client
\* Enumerate all possible client identifiers; for simplicity we bound it by M
M           \\ Maximum number of distinct clients  

ASSUME N >= 1
ASSUME M >= 1

VARIABLES allocated, client, free
(* Types *)

Pages   == {p | p : Nat & 1 <= p /\ p <= N}
Clients== {c | c : Nat & 1 <= c /\ c <= M}

TypeOK ==
    /\ free      \subseteq Pages
    /\ allocated \in [Client -> SUBSET Pages]
    /\ (\forall c \in Clients :
            (allocated[c] \subseteq Pages) /\
            (* No duplicate allocation across different clients *)   
            (\exists! x \in Pages : \/(\wedge i,j \in Clients:
                    i /= j => ~(x \in allocated[i]) /\ ~((i = c)/\(j=c)))))

(*
The last part ensures that each page appears in at most one client's set.
*)

(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
(* Initial state: no allocations and every page is free. *)

Init ==
    /\ free        = Pages
    /\ allocated' = [c \in Clients |-> {}]

Next ==
\* A request to allocate a single free page for client `req`
/\ reqReq? =>
     LET availablePage IN CHOOSE p \in free |
         TRUE
     BECAUSE availablePage # NULL
     THEN 
          /\ free           := free - <<availablePage>>
          /\ allocated'[req]:= allocated[req] #<<availablePage>>  


\/ Release of all pages held by client rel back into the pool.
/\ relRel?
   /\ releasedPages  = allocated[rel]
   /\ free      ':= free U releasedPages
   /\ allocated'[rel]:= {}

\/ Termination disjunction (to avoid deadlock)
\/ Terminating
   /\ UNCHANGED vars

Spec == Init /\ []<>(vars) \/ Next)_vars ???

---- MODULE MemoryAllocator ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N
Pages == 1..N
Clients == {"C1", "C2", "C3"}  \* Example client set

VARIABLE free, allocated

Init == /\ free = Pages
        /\ allocated = [c \in Clients |-> {}]

\* Allocate a page p to client c if p is free
Alloc(c, p) == /\ p \in free
                /\ free' = free \ {p}
                /\ allocated' = [allocated EXCEPT ![c] = @ \cup {p}]

\* Release a page p from client c
Release(c, p) == /\ p \in allocated[c]
                 /\ free' = free \cup {p}
                 /\ allocated' = [allocated EXCEPT ![c] = @ \ {p}]

Next == \E c \in Clients : \E p \in Pages :
          Alloc(c, p) \/ Release(c, p)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<free, allocated>>

\* Invariant: no page allocated to more than one client
TypeOK == \A p \in Pages: Cardinality({c \in Clients: p \in allocated[c]}) <= 1

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A single broker with subscribers and publishers. Subscribers register interest in topics. Publishers post messages on topics. The broker delivers each message to all registered subscribers.

Hints: Messages arrive in order per topic.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE Broker ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets, Sequences

CONSTANT Topics, SubIds, PubIds, MessageId
VARIABLES delivered, msgSeqs, subs


(* Types *)
TypeOk ==
    \/ subs : [Topic |-> SUBSET(SubIds)]
       /\\ msgSeqs : [Topic |-> Seq(Message)]          (* ordered list of messages yet undelivered *)
       /\\ delivered :
            [SubscriberId |
                [MessageId ->
                    BOOLEAN]]                         (* true iff this sub got that Msg *)

MsgRec == <<msgid: Nat,
           pubid: PubIds,
           payload: STRING>>

Init ==
    /\ subs = [{t \in Topics} t \mapsto {}]
    /\ msgSeqs = [{t \in Topics} t \mapsto []]     \* empty sequences
    /\ delivered =
        {sub \in SubIds |
             sub \mapsto {[m.msgid \in 1..0]: FALSE}}   \* no message ids known initially

Register(sub, topic) ==
    /\ sub \in SubIds
    /\ topic \in Topics
    /\ UNCHANGED <<pubids>>
    /\ subs' = [subs EXCEPT ![topic] = @ UNION {sub}]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<msgseqs,delivered>>  

Post(pub, topic, payld) ==
    /\ pub \in PubIds
    /\ topic \in Topics
    /\ let newID = IF Len(msgSeqs(topic))=0 THEN 1 ELSE (Last(msgSeqs(topic)).msgid)+1 in 
         \/ msgSeqs' = [msgSeqs EXCEPT ![topic]=Append(@,{<<newID,pub,payld>>}) ]
            /\\ subs' = subs          (* unchanged *)
            /\\ delivered' = delivered
       /\ UNCHANGED vars

DeliverOne ==
    LET topicsWithMsgs == {t \in Topics : Len(msgSeqs(t)) > 0}
    IN  
      /\ EXISTS t \in topicsWithMsgs :
           /\ subscribers := subs[t]
           /\ msgsToSend := Head(msgSeqs[t])
           /\ FORALL s \in subscribers:
                delivered'[s][msgsToSend.msgid] = TRUE
           /\ msgSeqs' = [msgSeqs EXCEPT ![t] = Tail(@)]
        /\ Unchanged(vars)

(* Termination disjunct to avoid deadlock when no actions possible *)    
Terminate ==
     /\ UNCHANGED <<subs,msgseqs,delivered>>

Next ==
   \/ Register(sub,\*sub\*,topic)              \\ placeholder for actual parameters
   \/ Post(pub,\*pub*\ , topic, payld)
   \/ DeliverOne
   \/ Terminate

Spec == Init /\ [] ( Next )_vars 

====

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Dekker's mutual exclusion algorithm for 2 processes, the first known correct solution to the problem.

Hints: VARIABLES turn, wants. Both safety and liveness should be modelled.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE DekkersAlgorithm ----

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT P1, P2
VARIABLES turn, want


(* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *)
\* Variables:
\*   - turn : {P1,P2}          -- whose turn it currently is  
\*   - want : [{{P1}, {P2}} -> BOOLEAN]   
\*
\* The array 'want'[p] indicates whether process p wishes to enter its critical section  

TypeOk ==
    /\ turn \in {{P1},{P2}}
    /\ want = <<TRUE/False>>? (* placeholder will refine below *)

Init ==
    /\ turn \in {{P1},{P2}}
    /\ want[P1] = FALSE
    /\ want[P2] = FALSE

Next ==
    \/ ProcessOneEntry
    \/ ProcessTwoEntry
    \/ ProcessOneExit
    /\\ Terminating      \* allow dead‑lock free termination if desired

ProcessOneEntry == 
    /\ want' = [[w IN {P1,P2}] |-> IF w=P1 THEN TRUE ELSE want[w]]
    /\ UNCHANGED turn
    /\ (turn'=P1)

ProcessTwoEntry == 
    /\ want' = [[w IN {P1,P2}] |-> IF w=P2 THEN TRUE ELSE want[w]]    
    /\ UNCHANGEd turn
    /\ (turn'=P2) 

(* Exiting processes set their flag back to false *)
ProcessOneExit  == 
    /\ want'[P1]=FALSE
    /\ UNCHANGED [turn, want[~P1]]

ProcessTwoExit ==  
    /\ want'[P2]=FALSE
    /\ UNCHANGED [turn,want[~P2]]

Terminating ==   
   /\ UNCHANGED <<want,turn>>

Spec == Init /\ [] Next  

====

Next == \/ /\ wants[0] = FALSE
           /\ wants[1] = FALSE
           /\ turn' = turn
           /\ wants' = wants
        \/ /\ wants[0] = FALSE
           /\ wants[1] = TRUE
           /\ turn' = 1
           /\ wants' = [wants EXCEPT ![0] = TRUE]
        \/ /\ wants[0] = TRUE
           /\ wants[1] = FALSE
           /\ turn' = 0
           /\ wants' = [wants EXCEPT ![1] = TRUE]
        \/ /\ wants[0] = TRUE
           /\ wants[1] = TRUE
           /\ turn' = turn
           /\ wants' = wants

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<turn, wants>>

          /\ wants \in [ {0,1} -> BOOLEAN ]

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A distributed grow-only counter (G-Counter CRDT) with N nodes. Each node increments its own slot. Merge takes element-wise max. All nodes eventually agree on the total.

Hints: VARIABLES counts[Node][Node]. Global count = sum of max(counts[][n]).

---- MODULE GCounter ---- EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets CONSTANT NodeSet \\ Set of all nodes assumed non-empty and finite, NodeSet CONSTDEF MaxVal \\ Upper bound for any individual increment per step VARIABLES counts \\ Matrix [node -> node] holding local counters, counts, m, n (* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *) \* --- Variable domains ------------------------------------------------- TypeOk == /\ counts : [NodeSet -> [NodeSet -> Nat]] /\ (\forall n1,n2 \in NodeSet : counts[n1][n2] <= MaxVal) Init == (* Initially all entries are zero *) /\ counts = <<>> \/ (\lambda _ \in NodeSet: (\lambda _ \in NodeSet: 0)) Next == LET inc(n) == (* Increment operation performed by node 'n' *) /\ UNCHANGED <> /\ counts'[n][n] = counts[n][n] + 1 merge(m,p) == (* Merge state from peer p into m's view *) /\ UNCHANGED <> /\ UNCHANGED <

> /\ (\forall i,j \in NodeSet : counts[i][j]' = IF j IN {i} THEN MAX(counts[m][j], counts[p][j]) term == (* Termination disjunction to avoid deadlock *) /\ UNCHANGED <> IN (inc(Node)) \/ (* STRING node may increment its own slot *) (merge(ANY, ANY)) /\ term (* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *) GlobalCount == SUM_{n \in NodeSet}(MAX(\{counts[][n]\})) Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<> ====

------------------------------- MODULE CRDT ---------------------------------

EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT Node
ASSUME NodeAssumption == IsFiniteSet(Node)

VARIABLE counter
vars == counter

TypeOK == counter \in [Node -> [Node -> Nat]]

Safety == \A n, o \in Node : counter[n][n] >= counter[o][n]

Monotonic == \A n, o \in Node : counter'[n][o] >= counter[n][o]

Monotonicity == [][Monotonic]_counter

\* Repeatedly, the counters at all nodes are in sync.
Convergence == []<>(\A n, o \in Node : counter[n] = counter[o])

Init == counter = [n \in Node |-> [o \in Node |-> 0]]

Increment(n) == counter' = [counter EXCEPT ![n][n] = @ + 1]

Gossip(n, o) ==
  LET Max(a, b) == IF a > b THEN a ELSE b IN
  counter' = [
    counter EXCEPT ![o] = [
      nodeView \in Node |->
        Max(counter[n][nodeView], counter[o][nodeView])
      ]
    ]

Next ==
  \/ \E n \in Node : Increment(n)
  \/ \E n, o \in Node : Gossip(n, o)

Spec ==
  /\ Init
  /\ [][Next]_counter


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* Fairness and liveness assumptions.                                      *)
(* We assume that Gossip actions will eventually occur when enabled, and   *)
(* that from some point onwards, only Gossip actions will be performed.    *)
(* In other words, incrementation of counters happens only finitely often. *)
(* Note that the second conjunct is not a standard fairness condition,     *)
(* yet the overall specification is machine closed.                        *)
(***************************************************************************)
Fairness ==
    /\ \A n, o \in Node : WF_vars(Gossip(n,o))
    /\ <>[][\E n, o \in Node : Gossip(n,o)]_vars

FairSpec ==
  /\ Spec
  /\ Fairness

=============================================================================

```tla
---- MODULE TwoPhaseCommit ----
EXTENDS Integers

CONSTANT N
ASSUME N \in 1..10

VARIABLES coordState, partState, decision

Parts == 1..N

TypeOK ==
    /\ coordState \in {"init", "waiting", "committed", "aborted"}
    /\ partState \in [Parts -> {"working", "prepared", "aborted", "committed"}]
    /\ decision \in {"none", "commit", "abort"}

Init ==
    /\ coordState = "init"
    /\ partState = [p \in Parts |-> "working"]
    /\ decision = "none"

Prepare ==
    /\ coordState = "init"
    /\ coordState' = "waiting"
    /\ UNCHANGED <<partState, decision>>

VoteYes(p) ==
    /\ coordState = "waiting"
    /\ partState[p] = "working"
    /\ partState' = [partState EXCEPT ![p] = "prepared"]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<coordState, decision>>

VoteNo(p) ==
    /\ coordState = "waiting"
    /\ partState[p] = "working"
    /\ partState' = [partState EXCEPT ![p] = "aborted"]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<coordState, decision>>

DecideCommit ==
    /\ coordState = "waiting"
    /\ \A p \in Parts : partState[p] = "prepared"
    /\ decision' = "commit"
    /\ coordState' = "committed"
    /\ UNCHANGED partState

DecideAbort ==
    /\ coordState = "waiting"
    /\ \E p \in Parts : partState[p] = "aborted"
    /\ decision' = "abort"
    /\ coordState' = "aborted"
    /\ UNCHANGED partState

CommitPart(p) ==
    /\ decision = "commit"
    /\ partState[p] = "prepared"
    /\ partState' = [partState EXCEPT ![p] = "committed"]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<coordState, decision>>

AbortPart(p) ==
    /\ decision = "abort"
    /\ partState[p] \in {"prepared", "working"}
    /\ partState' = [partState EXCEPT ![p] = "aborted"]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<coordState, decision>>

Done ==
    /\ coordState \in {"committed", "aborted"}
    /\ \A p \in Parts : partState[p] \in {"committed", "aborted"}
    /\ UNCHANGED <<coordState, partState, decision>>

Next ==
    \/ Prepare
    \/ \E p \in Parts : VoteYes(p) \/ VoteNo(p)
    \/ DecideCommit
    \/ DecideAbort
    \/ \E p \in Parts : CommitPart(p) \/ AbortPart(p)
    \/ Done

Consistency ==
    \A p \in Parts :
        partState[p] = "committed" => decision = "commit"

vars == <<coordState, partState, decision>>
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars
====
```

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Lamport's bakery mutual exclusion algorithm for N processes. Processes take a numbered ticket; lower numbers enter first.

```tla
---- MODULE BakeryAlgorithm ----
EXTENDS Integers, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N
ASSUME N \in 1..5

VARIABLES num, flag, pc

Procs == 1..N

TypeOK ==
    /\ num \in [Procs -> 0..20]
    /\ flag \in [Procs -> BOOLEAN]
    /\ pc \in [Procs -> {"idle", "doorway", "waiting", "critical"}]

Init ==
    /\ num = [p \in Procs |-> 0]
    /\ flag = [p \in Procs |-> FALSE]
    /\ pc = [p \in Procs |-> "idle"]

Max(S) == IF S = {} THEN 0
          ELSE CHOOSE x \in S : \A y \in S : x >= y

Doorway(p) ==
    /\ pc[p] = "idle"
    /\ Max({num[q] : q \in Procs}) < 18
    /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![p] = TRUE]
    /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![p] = Max({num[q] : q \in Procs}) + 1]
    /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![p] = "doorway"]

FinishDoorway(p) ==
    /\ pc[p] = "doorway"
    /\ flag' = [flag EXCEPT ![p] = FALSE]
    /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![p] = "waiting"]
    /\ UNCHANGED num

EnterCS(p) ==
    /\ pc[p] = "waiting"
    /\ \A q \in Procs \ {p} :
        /\ ~flag[q]
        /\ num[q] = 0 \/ num[p] < num[q] \/ (num[p] = num[q] /\ p < q)
    /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![p] = "critical"]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<num, flag>>

ExitCS(p) ==
    /\ pc[p] = "critical"
    /\ num' = [num EXCEPT ![p] = 0]
    /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT ![p] = "idle"]
    /\ UNCHANGED flag

Next == \E p \in Procs :
    Doorway(p) \/ FinishDoorway(p) \/ EnterCS(p) \/ ExitCS(p)

MutualExclusion ==
    \A p, q \in Procs :
        (p # q) => ~(pc[p] = "critical" /\ pc[q] = "critical")

vars == <<num, flag, pc>>
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars
```

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Reference (from tlaplus/Examples-style analysis of the target module; do not copy verbatim — use as guidance only):

## Protocol / intent
This specification formalizes The Boulangerie Algorithm as a TLA+ module. From the authors: ) The state space is organized around variables num, flag, pc, unchecked, max, nxt. SANY analysis identifies 11 action-level operators and 4 temporal-level operators. Weak fairness (WF_) on action(s).

## Algorithm (title)
The Boulangerie Algorithm

## Constants / parameters
N

## State variables
num, flag, pc, unchecked, max, nxt

## Init (SANY-style summary)
num = [0] /\ flag = [FALSE] /\ unchecked = [{}] /\ max = [0] /\ nxt = [1] /\ pc = ["ncs"]

## Actions (names + intent)
- **ncs**: Action-level operator (transitions involving primed variables).
  transition sketch: `pc[self] = "ncs" /\ pc' = [pc EXCEPT (Seq(self), "e1")] /\ UNCHANGED <<num, flag, unchecked, max, nxt>>`
- **e1**: Action-level operator (transitions involving primed variables).

A database with snapshot isolation. Transactions read a consistent snapshot. Write-write conflicts cause an abort.
broken_spec:
```tla
---- MODULE SnapshotIsolation ----
EXTENDS Integers, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N
ASSUME N \in 1..3

Keys == 1..N
Txns == 1..N

VARIABLES db, txState, txSnapshot, txWrites

TypeOK ==
    /\ db \in [Keys -> 0..5]
    /\ txState \in [Txns -> {"idle", "active", "committed", "aborted"}]
    /\ txSnapshot \in [Txns -> [Keys -> 0..5]]
    /\ txWrites \in [Txns -> SUBSET Keys]

Init ==
    /\ db = [k \in Keys |-> 0]
    /\ txState = [t \in Txns |-> "idle"]
    /\ txSnapshot = [t \in Txns |-> [k \in Keys |-> 0]]
    /\ txWrites = [t \in Txns |-> {}]

Begin(t) ==
    /\ txState[t] = "idle"
    /\ txState' = [txState EXCEPT ![t] = "active"]
    /\ txSnapshot' = [txSnapshot EXCEPT ![t] = db]
    /\ txWrites' = [txWrites EXCEPT ![t] = {}]
    /\ UNCHANGED db

Write(t, k) ==
    /\ txState[t] = "active"
    /\ k \in Keys
    /\ txWrites' = [txWrites EXCEPT ![t] = @ \cup {k}]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<db, txState, txSnapshot>>

Commit(t) ==
    /\ txState[t] = "active"
    /\ \A t2 \in Txns :
        (t2 # t /\ txState[t2] = "committed")
            => txWrites[t] \cap txWrites[t2] = {}
    /\ db' = [k \in Keys |->
        IF k \in txWrites[t] THEN txSnapshot[t][k] + 1 ELSE db[k]]
    /\ txState' = [txState EXCEPT ![t] = "committed"]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<txSnapshot, txWrites>>

Abort(t) ==
    /\ txState[t] = "active"
    /\ txState' = [txState EXCEPT ![t] = "aborted"]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<db, txSnapshot, txWrites>>

Done ==
    /\ \A t \in Txns : txState[t] \in {"committed", "aborted"}
    /\ UNCHANGED <<db, txState, txSnapshot, txWrites>>

Next == (\E t \in Txns :
    Begin(t) \/ (\E k \in Keys : Write(t, k)) \/ Commit(t) \/ Abort(t)) \/ Done

NoWriteConflict ==
    \A t1, t2 \in Txns :
        (t1 # t2 /\ txState[t1] = "committed" /\ txState[t2] = "committed")
            => txWrites[t1] \cap txWrites[t2] = {}
```

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

N nodes exchange clock values to synchronise. After one round, all clocks are within epsilon of each other.
broken_spec:
<think>Repairing the module.</think>
---- MODULE ClockSync ----
EXTENDS Integers

CONSTANT N
ASSUME N \in 1..5

VARIABLES clock, synced

Nodes == 1..N

TypeOK ==
    /\ clock \in [Nodes -> 0..10]
    /\ synced \in [Nodes -> BOOLEAN]

Init ==
    /\ clock \in [Nodes -> 0..3]
    /\ synced = [n \in Nodes |-> FALSE]

Sync(i) ==
    /\ i \in Nodes
    /\ ~synced[i]
    /\ \E j \in Nodes :
        /\ j # i
        /\ LET avg == (clock[i] + clock[j]) \div 2
           IN clock' = [clock EXCEPT ![i] = avg]
    /\ synced' = [synced EXCEPT ![i] = TRUE]

Done ==
    /\ \A n \in Nodes : synced[n]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<clock, synced>>

Next == (\E i \in Nodes : Sync(i)) \/ Done

ClockBound ==
    (\A n \in Nodes : synced[n])
        => \A i, j \in Nodes : clock[i] - clock[j] \in -5..5

vars == <<clock, synced>>
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars
====

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

A single broker with subscribers and publishers. Subscribers register interest in topics. Publishers post messages on topics. The broker delivers each message to all registered subscribers.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE PubSubBroker ----
---- MODULE PubSubBroker ----
EXTENDS Integers, FiniteSets

CONSTANT N
ASSUME N \in 1..3

Topics == 1..N
Subs == 1..N
MaxMsgs == 1

VARIABLES subscriptions, published, delivered

TypeOK ==
    /\ subscriptions \in [Subs -> SUBSET Topics]
    /\ published \in [Topics -> 0..3]
    /\ delivered \in [Subs -> [Topics -> 0..3]]

Init ==
    /\ subscriptions = [s \in Subs |-> {}]
    /\ published = [t \in Topics |-> 0]
    /\ delivered = [s \in Subs |-> [t \in Topics |-> 0]]

Subscribe(s, t) ==
    /\ s \in Subs
    /\ t \in Topics
    /\ subscriptions' = [subscriptions EXCEPT ![s] = @ \cup {t}]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<published, delivered>>

Publish(t) ==
    /\ t \in Topics
    /\ published[t] < MaxMsgs
    /\ published' = [published EXCEPT ![t] = @ + 1]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<subscriptions, delivered>>

Deliver(s, t) ==
    /\ s \in Subs
    /\ t \in subscriptions[s]
    /\ delivered[s][t] < published[t]
    /\ delivered' = [delivered EXCEPT ![s][t] = @ + 1]
    /\ UNCHANGED <<subscriptions, published>>

Done ==
    /\ \A t \in Topics : published[t] = MaxMsgs
    /\ UNCHANGED <<subscriptions, published, delivered>>

Next ==
    \/ \E s \in Subs, t \in Topics : Subscribe(s, t)
    \/ \E t \in Topics : Publish(t)
    \/ \E s \in Subs, t \in Topics : Deliver(s, t)
    \/ Done

DeliveryGuarantee ==
    \A s \in Subs, t \in Topics :
        t \in subscriptions[s] => delivered[s][t] <= published[t]

vars == <<subscriptions, published, delivered>>
Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars
====

nl: A lease-based mutual exclusion protocol. A node acquires a time-limited lease. Other nodes wait until the lease expires.
broken_spec:
<think>Repairing the module.</think>
---- MODULE LeaseProtocol ----
EXTENDS Integers, Sequences, FiniteSets, TLC

CONSTANTS N, T  \* Number of nodes and lease duration

VARIABLES owner, lease, time

(* owner: node that holds the lease or 0 if none
   lease: remaining lease time (0 if no lease)
   time: global time counter (for simulation) *)

(* Helper to get the set of nodes *)
Nodes == 1..N

(* Initial state: no owner, no lease, time 0 *)
Init == /\ owner = 0
        /\ lease = 0
        /\ time = 0

(* Action: a node requests the lease *)
Request(i) ==
  /\ i \in Nodes
  /\ owner = 0
  /\ lease = 0
  /\ owner' = i
  /\ lease' = T
  /\ time' = time

(* Action: the lease expires *)
Expire ==
  /\ lease > 0
  /\ lease' = lease - 1
  /\ owner' = IF lease' = 0 THEN 0 ELSE owner
  /\ time' = time

(* Action: a node releases the lease early *)
Release(i) ==
  /\ i = owner
  /\ lease = 0
  /\ owner' = 0
  /\ time' = time

(* Next-state relation *)
Next == \/ \E i \in Nodes : Request(i)
        \/ Expire
        \/ \E i \in Nodes : Release(i)

(* Invariant: owner is either 0 or a valid node *)
TypeOK == owner \in 0..N /\ lease \in 0..T /\ time \in Nat

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_<<owner, lease, time>>

====

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

N-thread barrier: each thread arrives, increments count, blocks until count = N, then all proceed and count resets. Safety: any thread is past the barrier only if all N have arrived.
broken_spec:
```tla
---- MODULE Barrier ----
EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

CONSTANT Procs

\* arrived = set of processes currently waiting at the barrier
\* past    = set of processes that have crossed the barrier this round
VARIABLES arrived, past

vars == << arrived, past >>

N == Cardinality(Procs)

Init == /\ arrived = {}
        /\ past    = {}

\* A process not yet at the barrier arrives.
Arrive(p) == /\ p \notin arrived
             /\ p \notin past
             /\ arrived' = arrived \cup {p}
             /\ UNCHANGED past

\* When all N processes have arrived, the barrier opens: they all become past.
Release == /\ Cardinality(arrived) = N
           /\ past' = arrived
           /\ arrived' = {}

\* New round: once all are past, reset for the next iteration.
Reset == /\ Cardinality(past) = N
         /\ past' = {}
         /\ UNCHANGED arrived

Next == \/ \E p \in Procs : Arrive(p)
        \/ Release
        \/ Reset

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

\* Safety: no process is past the barrier unless all N have arrived
\* this round. Equivalently, past is either empty or has exactly N members.
BarrierSafe == /\ (past = {}) \/ (Cardinality(past) = N)
               /\ (arrived \cap past) = {}

TypeOK == /\ arrived \subseteq Procs
          /\ past    \subseteq Procs
          /\ BarrierSafe
====

```

nl:
Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Cigarette smokers problem with one agent and three smokers. Safety: a smoker smokes only when it has all three ingredients.
broken_spec:
---- MODULE CigaretteSmokers ----
---- MODULE CigaretteSmokers ----
(***************************************************************************)
(* The cigarette smokers problem (Patil, 1971).                            *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Three smokers each have an infinite supply of one ingredient            *)
(* (tobacco / paper / matches).  An agent puts the OTHER TWO ingredients   *)
(* on the table the smoker who has the missing third one then smokes.    *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Safety: a smoker is "smoking" only when both ingredients it lacks are  *)
(* present on the table.                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
EXTENDS Naturals, FiniteSets

\* Smoker i owns ingredient i, lacks the other two.
Smokers == {0, 1, 2}
Ingredients == {0, 1, 2}

OwnedBy(s) == s              \* smoker s owns ingredient s
Lacks(s)   == Ingredients \ {s}

VARIABLES table, smoking

vars == << table, smoking >>

NoSmoker == 3

Init == /\ table = {}        \* nothing on the table
        /\ smoking = NoSmoker

\* Agent puts ingredients i and j on the table (the third smoker will smoke).
\* Only fires when the table is empty and no one is smoking.
AgentPut == /\ table = {}
            /\ smoking = NoSmoker
            /\ \E i, j \in Ingredients :
                 /\ i # j
                 /\ table' = {i, j}
                 /\ UNCHANGED smoking

\* Smoker s grabs both ingredients it lacks and starts smoking.
StartSmoke(s) == /\ smoking = NoSmoker
                 /\ Lacks(s) \subseteq table
                 /\ table' = table \ Lacks(s)
                 /\ smoking' = s

\* Finish smoking — table empty, smoker idle.
FinishSmoke == /\ smoking # NoSmoker
               /\ smoking' = NoSmoker
               /\ UNCHANGED table

Next == AgentPut \/ (\E s \in Smokers : StartSmoke(s)) \/ FinishSmoke

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_vars

\* Strong safety: at most one smoker is smoking, and a smoker is only
\* smoking after having actually grabbed the missing ingredients
\* (encoded by the table being emptied of those ingredients).
SmokerInv == (smoking = NoSmoker) \/ (smoking \in Smokers /\ table = {})

TypeOK == /\ table \subseteq Ingredients /\ smoking \in Smokers \cup {NoSmoker} /\ SmokerInv
====

Write a TLA+ specification for the following:

Counting semaphore with capacity K. Acquire decrements (blocks at 0), release increments (capped at K). Safety: count stays in 0..K and acquired processes is bounded.
